summary: Excellent soundboard recording of a landmark 1974 show highlighted by a monster Eyes of the World and the legendary Playin > Scarlet > Playin' sandwich.
review: The performance is consistently praised as one of the year’s best, with an explosive first set driven by a 19‑minute Eyes of the World and a 37‑minute Playin > Scarlet > Playin' jam. The soundboard source is described as crisp, clear and A+ quality, though a few listeners note a slightly flat vocal mix and tinny drum tone. Overall the show is deemed essential for any Deadhead collection.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews, almost all 5‑star, uniformly praise both musical performance and recording fidelity; minor technical complaints do not outweigh the overwhelming enthusiasm.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with strong instrument separation. A few comments about slightly flat vocal tones and a tinny drum sound, but overall regarded as one of the best‑sounding SBDs of 1974.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is a tour‑de‑force – tight pacing, no dead air, and the Playin > Scarlet sandwich serves as an unforgettable climax. Second set maintains high energy with strong jams (UJB, Sugar Magnolia/Truckin’/Spanish Jam/Other One) though a few feel it’s slightly overrated compared to the first set.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly lauded for scorching guitar tone, lead work and “lightning‑bolt” solos; his vocals are also praised as among the best of the era.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as phat, sharp and prominently featured in the mix with powerful bass bombs that drive the jams.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals noted as top‑notch for this period; Bobby’s onstage banter (fence‑climbing warning) adds character.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s keyboards highlighted for reeling Jerry back in during the Playin > Scarlet jam and providing a groovy, melodic undercurrent.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann receives credit for dynamic drum rolls and propulsion; some listeners mention a slightly tinny drum sound on the recording.
